original: Smallest dinosaur ever discovered - skull preserved in piece of amber smaller than a fingertip from a mine in Myanmar, reported in "Nature"
Astronomers announce the discovery of 128 new moons orbiting the planet Saturn, bring the planet's total to 274 moons
NASA's space telescope, the Spherex observatory, is launched by SpaceX from California to study how galaxies are formed by observing their cosmic glow
John Smith, US amateur wrestler, wins James E Sullivan Award
Nicholas Winton (93), former British stockbroker who helped 669 young Jews flee Czechoslovakia on the eve of the Nazi invasion in 1939, receives knighthood from Queen Elizabeth at Buckingham Palace
1st public basketball game (Springfield, Massachusetts)
Utrecht conservatory destroyed by fire
Yolanda Chen hop-skip-jumps world indoor record 15.03m
Ice Dance Championship at Helsinki, Finland won byJayne TorvillandChristopher Dean(Great Britain)
NFL adopts instant replay rule
Gene Roddenberrybrings together a 16-page pitch for the original Star Trek series
Ezra Jack Keats, American children's author known for "The Snowy Day" winner of the 1963 Caldecott Medal, born in Brooklyn, New York (d. 1983)
Rob Paulsen, American voice actor (Pinky and The Brain), born in Detroit, Michigan

Becky Hammon, American Basketball HOF point guard (WNBA: NY Liberty, SA Stars; Russia Olympics 2008, 12) and coach (first female to coach an NBA team; SA Spurs 2020; LV Aces), born in Rapid City, South Dakota
The West first learns of the Giant Panda via French missionary Armand David who receives a skin from a hunter
Randy Hold receives 67 min in penalties in a 60 min NHL hockey game
Robert L. Carter, American civil rights activist and judge (Brown v. Board of Education, NAACP v. Alabama), born in Caryville, Florida (d. 2012)
Thomas Starzl, American surgeon, performed 1st successful liver transplant, born in Le Mars, Iowa (d. 2017)
Mt Etna in Sicily erupts in its largest recorded eruption, killing 15,000
Great blizzard of '88 strikes northeastern USA
A meteorite enters the earth's atmosphere and explodes over New Martinsville, West Virginia. The debris causes damage but no human injuries are reported.
Johnny Mize and Rube Foster are elected to the Baseball Hall of Fame
Japanese probe Sakigake flies by Halley's Comet at 6.8 million km
132-year old shipwreck rediscovered in Lake Superior of steel streamer "The Great Western" which sank in 1892 with the loss of 27 lives
Norwegian scientist and explorerFridtjof Nansensets out on his first expedition on a sea voyage to study Arctic zoology
Lithuania declares its Independence from the Soviet Union
Bobby McFerrin, American Grammy Award-winning vocalist ("Don't Worry Be Happy"), and classical music conductor, born in New York City
Curtis Brown, American NASA astronaut (STS 47, STS 66, 77, 85, 95), born in Elizabethtown, North Carolina
Buvaisar Saitiev, Russian freestyle wrestler (Olympic gold 74kg 1996, 2004, 08; World C'ship gold x 6), born in Khasavyurt, Russia (d. 2025)
Arooj Aftab, Pakistani-American singer and composer (Mohabbat), born in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia
Tammy Beaumont, English cricket batter (Test HS 208 v Aus 2023 highest by Englishwoman in Test cricket; Kent CC), born in Dover, England
Johann Sebastian Bach's "St Matthew Passion" is revived byFelix Mendelssohn, aged 20, conducting in Berlin, Germany
Urbain Jean Joseph le Verrier, French astronomer (made calculations that proved existence of Neptune), born in Saint-Lô, France (d. 1877)
Women's Medical College of Pennsylvania opens, 2nd female medical school in the US
Cincinnati Enquirer reports Baltimore manager John McGraw signed Cherokee Indian Tokohoma, who is really black 2nd baseman Charlie Grant
Conservationists John Merriam, Madison Grant, and Henry Fairfield Osborn found "Save the Redwoods League" - a non-profit forest conservation land trust in San Francisco, California founded
Pioneer 5 launched into solar orbit between Earth & Venus
Rhino Records store in Claremont, California offers customers 5 cents to take home "Partridge Family" actor Danny Bonaduce's debut solo album
NBA suspends 2019-20 season until further notice after Utah Jazz center Rudy Gobert tests positive for COVID-19
Benjamin BannekerandPierre Charles L'Enfantbegin to lay out Washington, D.C.
Episode of "The Monkees" TV show features musicianFrank Zappaposing as Mike Nesmith and vice versa, debating qualities of each other's music
BeatlePaul McCartneyis knighted byQueen Elizabeth II
Superhero movie "The Black Panther" becomes the fifthMarvelmovie to earn $1 billion worldwide
Otto Friedrich Müller, Danish Naturalist who was a pioneer in the study of microorganisms including bacteria, diatoms, and infusoria, born in Copenhagen, Denmark (d. 1784)
Louis Bachelier, French mathematician who pioneered mathematical finance introducing Brownian motion, born in Le Havre, France (d. 1946)
Vannevar Bush, American engineer and science administrator (developed 1st electronic analogue computer), born in Everett, Massachusetts (d. 1974)
Eleni Gatzoyiannis, Greek heroine (subject of "Eleni" by son Nicholas Gage about how she saved her children), born in Greece (d. 1948)
Fitzroy Maclean, 1st Baronet, Scottish adventurer, soldier, writer and politician who was an inspiration for Ian Flemings James Bond, born in Cairo, Khedivate of Egypt, Ottoman Empire (d. 1996)
Malcolm Wells, American architect regarded as "the father of modern earth-sheltered architecture", writer, and solar energy consultant, born in Camden, New Jersey (d. 2009)[1]
Anton Yelchin, Russian-born American actor (Star Trek), born in Leningrad, Soviet Union (d. 2016)
Otis Reddingis the first person in the US to posthumously receive gold record for his single "(Sittin' On) The Dock of the Bay"
1st NHL championship game ever played, Toronto Arenas beats Montreal Canadiens 7-3 in 1st of 2 game set (second game on March 13)
1st woman army doctor commissioned (FM Adams)
Mount Etna in Sicily erupted
J. C. R. Licklider, American computer scientist and Internet pioneer, born in St. Louis Missouri (d. 1990)
Astor Piazzolla, Argentinian  bandoneon player, and composer (Tango Nuevo), born in Mar del Plata, Argentina (d. 1992)
Alexandra Zabelina, Russian fencer (Olympic gold USSR women's team foil 1960, 68, 72), born in Moscow, Russia (d. 2022)
(Leonardo) "Flaco" Jiménez, American Grammy Lifetime Achievement Award-winning Tex-Mex and conjunto accordionist (Texas Tornados), born in San Antonio, Texas (d. 2025)[1][2]
Dock Ellis, American baseball pitcher (MLB All-Star, World Series 1971 Pittsburgh Pirates; pitched no-hitter 1971), born in Los Angeles, California (d. 2008)
Jerry Zucker, American director and producer (Airplane!; The Naked Gun; Ghost), born in Milwaukee, Wisconsin[1]
